persistence.batching_rule: Checkpoint queued state after several questions for flow; formally persist D-231 through D-240 at D-240 unless an earlier handoff is requested

constraints: Stage 1 and Stage 2 are divided into bounded evidence-producing milestones rather than one open-ended stage deadline
Each milestone receives a predeclared effort or calendar budget set before implementation as a planning variable
Reaching a cap pauses work for written evidence review diagnosis scope reduction resequencing extension or stop decisions
Extensions require a specific dependency or evidence-based reason a revised cap and unchanged acceptance evidence
Polish architecture generalization and sunk-cost pressure do not justify silently moving the gate
Preserve core mechanics representative content readability controls diagnostics valid test conditions and gate-relevant save or performance reliability
Cut additional weapons enemies rooms variants cosmetic polish secondary progression economy depth optional tools generalized architecture and premature post-MVP preparation first
Every cut is recorded with its reason and re-entry condition
Deferred scope cannot silently return during the same milestone
Validation accessibility diagnostics and reliability are not sacrificed merely to preserve visible feature count
Record the product question required evidence pass fail and technically-invalid outcomes included and excluded scope measurement method test population and approvers before work begins
The core question cannot be softened after development or evidence collection starts
Clarifications are allowed only before affected evidence is collected while material changes create a new version or test round
Previous results remain attached to their original rules and threshold changes require measurement-validity justification rather than disappointing results
Technical failures remain distinct from fun evidence and scope cuts must still answer the original question
